home.title: Service Catalogue
home.subtitle: The main entry point after sign-in. Pick the available application modules based on the active installation.
home.card.service_catalogue.title: Service Catalogue
home.card.service_catalogue.desc: Overview of services, KPIs, and navigation to the service catalogue list and graph.
home.card.c3_taxonomy.title: C3 Taxonomy
home.card.c3_taxonomy.desc: Dashboard for the C3 taxonomy, categories, and navigation to C3 lists.
home.card.c3_dashboard.title: C3 Dashboard
home.card.c3_dashboard.desc: Visualization of the C3 Capability Map.
administration.title: Administration
administration.subtitle: Manage users, reference data, audit, and operational application settings.
administration.card.users.title: User Management
administration.card.users.desc: Create users, assign User / Content Admin / Admin roles, and manage AD / SSO accounts.
administration.card.groups.title: Group Management
administration.card.groups.desc: Groups define access rights to Service Catalogue and C3 Taxonomy columns and dictionaries.
administration.card.web.title: Web
administration.card.web.desc: Configure ADFS / SSO trusted-header login and domain header mapping.
administration.card.import_history.title: Import History
administration.card.import_history.desc: Browse import batches, issue counts, and import execution details.
administration.card.import_audit.title: Import Audit
administration.card.import_audit.desc: Review import audit details, relation parsing outputs, and troubleshooting data.
administration.card.audit_logs.title: Audit Logs
administration.card.installation.title: Installation and Modules
administration.card.installation.desc: Installation status, active modules, repair flow, and release/schema handshake checks.
administration.card.catalogue_ref.title: Catalogue Reference Editing
administration.card.catalogue_ref.desc: Edit Service Catalogue reference dictionaries: Service Type, Status, Relation Type, Portfolio Group, and more.
administration.card.c3_ref.title: C3 Reference Editing
administration.card.c3_ref.desc: Edit C3 Taxonomy reference dictionaries, including C3 Mapping Type and C3 Capability Domain.
administration.card.c3_builder.title: C3 Capability Builder
administration.card.c3_builder.desc: Edit the final C3 Capability Map and the poster-seed-generated page title.
administration.users.provider.local.help: Sign in with username and password.
administration.users.provider.ad.label: AD / SSO
administration.users.provider.ad.help: Domain sign-in through trusted headers from a reverse proxy or IIS.
administration.users.title: Users
administration.users.ad_signin_note: AD/SSO sign-in works only for accounts of type `AD / SSO` that exist in the application and are active.
administration.users.search_placeholder: Filter by username, role, email, or AD identity
administration.web.setting.auth.sso.head: Identity header
administration.web.checkbox_label: Allow automatic domain sign-in through trusted headers
install.page.reset_confirm: Do you really want to reset the installation? This will cancel the in-progress install and the wizard will start again from step 1. The admin account and DB data will not be affected.
install.page.status_load_failed: Failed to load installation status. Verify that the middleware is running.
install.page.config_load_failed: Failed to save configuration. Verify middleware availability.
install.page.admin.create_error: Error while creating the admin account.
install.page.repair_mode_alert: The previous installation failed. This wizard will repair the incomplete state.
install.page.upgrade_mode_alert: The application version is newer than the version in the database. A migration will be performed.
install.page.modules.c3_desc: Capability Taxonomy module — C3 entities, capability map, technology relations, and the C3 dashboard. Optional and can be added after installation.
install.page.data_plan.core_installation: DB schema, indexes, constraints, system roles, and default configuration
install.page.data_plan.reference_seed_sc: Enums, system dashboard definitions, configuration taxonomy, editor metadata
install.page.data_plan.business_data_imp: Import in the optional step or later from the admin section
install.page.data_plan.demo_data_descrip: Creates 3 fully populated demo services (PIS, IAM, DAP) with SLAs, pricing, roles, relations, and mapped C3 taxonomy entities. Service names are marked with the [DEMO] prefix for easy identification.
install.page.data_plan.demo_data_note: Demo data will be created automatically during installation.
install.page.data_plan.core_reference_no: Core and reference seeds are applied automatically. Business data can be uploaded in the next step or skipped.
install.page.execute.import_skipped_miss: Import after installation was skipped because this wizard run did not create a new admin account with available credentials.
install.page.execute.import_login: Signing in the admin account for data import...
install.page.execute.import_login_failed: ⚠️ Sign-in for import failed — data can be imported from the admin section
install.page.execute.import_failed_gener: ⚠️ Import failed — data can be imported from the admin section
install.page.execute.log.prepare_demo_da: Preparing demo data (3 demo services + C3 links)...
install.page.execute.log.import_skipped_: ⚠️ Import after installation was skipped because this wizard run did not create a new admin account with available credentials.
install.page.execute.log.import_login: Signing in the admin account for data import...
install.page.execute.log.import_login_fa: ⚠️ Sign-in for import failed — data can be imported from the admin section
install.page.execute.log.import_failed_g: ⚠️ Import failed — data can be imported from the admin section
install.page.config.https_mode: HTTPS mode - force HTTPS in generated links
install.page.config.secrets_note: 🔒 Secrets are never displayed in plain text. Configuration will be stored in DB (app_config).
install.page.secrets.note: Secrets are validated by the middleware on startup. We only show validation results, never the secret values themselves.
install.page.secrets.passwords_note: 🔒 Passwords and tokens must never be stored in plaintext, source code, or the image. We recommend Docker Compose secrets mounted as /run/secrets/<name>.
install.page.admin.password_policy: 🔒 The password must be at least 10 characters long and contain uppercase letters, lowercase letters, digits, and a special character.
install.page.admin.require_password_chan: Require password change after first sign-in
install.page.connectivity.title: Connectivity test
install.page.connectivity.subtitle: We verify database reachability, write permissions, and the presence of the platform schema.
install.page.modules.title: Module activation
install.page.modules.subtitle: Service Catalogue Core is mandatory and cannot be disabled. The C3 Taxonomy module is optional and can be activated now or later in the admin section.
install.page.modules.core_desc: The application core: service list, detail, editor, import, dashboard, relations, and governance. Cannot be disabled.
install.page.modules.c3_desc: Capability Taxonomy module — C3 entities, capability map, technology relations, and the C3 dashboard. Optional and can be added after installation.
install.page.data_plan.title: Data packages
install.page.data_plan.subtitle: Data installation is split into three layers. Core and reference seeds are mandatory. Business data are optional and can be imported at any time.
install.page.data_plan.business_data_imp: Import in the optional step or later from the admin section
install.page.data_plan.demo_data_descrip: Creates 3 fully populated demo services (PIS, IAM, DAP) with SLAs, pricing, roles, relations, and mapped C3 taxonomy entities. Service names are marked with the [DEMO] prefix for easy identification.
install.page.data_plan.demo_data_note: Demo data will be created automatically during installation.
install.page.data_plan.core_reference_no: Core and reference seeds are applied automatically. Business data can be uploaded in the next step or skipped.
install.page.upload.title: Business data import (optional)
install.page.upload.subtitle: Upload one or more files with services. Each file is processed as a separate batch. This step can be skipped and the import can be done later from the admin section.
install.page.upload.drop_files: Drop files here or click to choose
install.page.upload.hint: CSV, XLSX, or JSON · Multiple files at once · Max 50 MB per file
install.page.upload.import_batch_note: Each file is processed as a separate import batch. Records with the same service_id are updated (upsert), and new records are inserted.
install.page.preview.analysis: Analysis of {{count}} {{noun}} — record counts, columns, warnings.
install.page.preview.no_files: No files to import. Continuing to installation.
install.page.preview.import_skipped: Import skipped. The system will be initialized with empty business data. You can import later from the admin section.
install.page.preview.ready_for_import_on: ✅ {{count}} file is ready for import — {{rows}} records will be processed after installation.
install.page.preview.ready_for_import_ma: ✅ {{count}} files are ready for import — {{rows}} records will be processed after installation.
install.page.preview.failed_one: {{count}} file failed to load and will be skipped. You can import it later from the admin section.
install.page.preview.failed_many: {{count}} files failed to load and will be skipped. You can import them later from the admin section.
install.page.execute.title: Run installation
install.page.execute.subtitle: Everything is ready. Installation will activate modules, persist configuration, and switch the system to READY.
install.page.execute.import_skipped: Skipped — data can be imported later in the admin section
install.page.result.ready_desc: The system is in READY state. The application is ready for operation.
install.page.result.failed_desc: Check the errors and start the repair flow.
install.page.result.warning: This page will not be shown again as the default entry after you leave it. Installation and module management remains available from the admin section → Installation & Modules.
install.page.result.summary_import_skipp: Skipped — data can be imported later in the admin section
install.page.welcome_subtitle: This wizard will guide you through the first run of the application. I detected the installation mode and prepared the required steps. The full process takes about 5–10 minutes.
install.page.setup_token_placeholder: Paste the setup token if the backend requires it
install.page.setup_token_hint: The token is sent only to mutating install endpoints. Without it, the secure bootstrap stays locked.
install.page.reset_description: The installation is incomplete. If the wizard stops working correctly (for example after a restart), it can be safely reset and started again. Existing database data will not be deleted.
install.page.config_subtitle: Set the core runtime parameters. Secrets (JWT, DB) are loaded from Compose environment and mounted secrets, so they cannot be changed here.
install.page.secrets_subtitle: Check that required secrets are provided through a safe channel (Compose secrets or env).
install.page.admin_subtitle: Enter credentials for the first system administrator. The password will be stored as a secure hash, never in plain text.
install.page.admin_exists_warning: An active admin account already exists in the database. This wizard is intended for a clean installation.
install.page.connectivity_subtitle: Verify database availability and write access before continuing the installation.
install.page.start_rate_limit: Too many requests. Try again in 30 seconds. If the issue persists, restart the middleware.
install.page.start_locked: The installation is locked from a previous attempt. Click "Reset installation" below and try again.
install.page.reset_error: Error while resetting the installation. Verify middleware availability.
auth.login.subtitle: Continue to sign in
auth.login.sso_denied: The domain account was recognized, but no active application account exists for it.
user_info.title: User Info
auth.errors.expired_token: Token expired, please sign in again
auth.errors.password_change_required: The first administrator must change their password.
auth.rate_limit.login: Too many sign-in attempts. Try again in 15 minutes.
install.rate_limit: Too many installation requests. Try again in a moment.
install.errors.setup_token_required: The setup token is missing or invalid.
install.errors.db_unavailable: The database is not yet available or has not been initialized.
install.errors.already_ready: Installation is already complete. Use the admin section for repair or upgrade.
